---
title: "Further Resources for Data Visualisation in R"
output: html_document
---

# Further Resources

## ggplot2 Resources

A single chapter in a book can never hope to cover all the available features and plot types available in `ggplot2`. Here are some essential resources to deepen your skills:

- The **[ggplot2 documentation](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/)** is comprehensive and includes examples for every function and geom.
- The **[R Graph Gallery](https://r-graph-gallery.com/)** shows hundreds of chart types with reproducible R code — an excellent reference for finding the right visualisation for your data.
- **[ggplot2: Elegant Graphics for Data Analysis](https://ggplot2-book.org/)** by Hadley Wickham is the definitive textbook on ggplot2, freely available online.

## ggplot2 Extensions

The `ggplot2` ecosystem has a rich set of extension packages that add new geoms, themes, and capabilities:

- **[ggplot2 extensions gallery](https://exts.ggplot2.tidyverse.org/)** — a curated catalogue of community-contributed extensions.
- **[patchwork](https://patchwork.data-imaginist.com/)** — compose multiple ggplot2 panels into a single figure with a simple, intuitive API.
- **[ggrepel](https://ggrepel.slowkow.com/)** — automatically repositions text labels to avoid overlapping.
- **[gganimate](https://gganimate.com/)** — add animation to ggplot2 figures.
- **[plotly](https://plotly.com/r/)** — convert ggplot2 figures into interactive, web-based charts with a single call to `ggplotly()`.

## Interactive Visualisation

For web-embedded, fully interactive charts:

- **[plotly for R](https://plotly.com/r/)** — create interactive charts natively in R or convert existing ggplot2 figures.
- **[leaflet](https://rstudio.github.io/leaflet/)** — interactive maps in R.
- **[highcharter](https://jkunst.com/highcharter/)** — a wrapper for the Highcharts JavaScript library.

## Learning Further

- **[R for Data Science (2nd ed.)](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/)** by Hadley Wickham, Mine Çetinkaya-Rundel, and Garrett Grolemund covers the full tidyverse workflow including visualisation.
- **[Fundamentals of Data Visualization](https://clauswilke.com/dataviz/)** by Claus Wilke — a language-agnostic guide to effective data visualisation, with all examples in R/ggplot2.
